info.title: OMS Fill Object File Service
info.description: The Fill Object File API allows you to fill the object file.

port.description: The port to which the server is listening

/fillObject.description: Use this path to fill the object file.

post.description: Use this operation to fill the object file.

messages.messageText: 1 objects added, 0 changed and 0 removed for environment *DEV &N Cause . . . . . : The fill object file you requested has been processed for environment *DEV. 1 objects have been added, 0 objects have been changed and 0 objects have been removed from the database. &N Recovery. . . . : No recovery necessary. This is an informational message.

The application code XXXXX does not exist. &N Cause . . . . . :   You have specified the application code XXXXX that does not exist. &N Recovery  . . . :   Specify the existing application code and try your request again.

/jobStatus.summary: Use this path to retrieves Fill Object File job status information.
/jobStatus.description: Use this path to retrieves Fill Object File job status information, including active jobs, jobs waiting on job queues, and completed jobs with output on output queues.

get.summary: Use this operation to retrieves Fill Object File job status information.
get.description: Use this operation to retrieves Fill Object File job status information, including active jobs, jobs waiting on job queues, and completed jobs with output on output queues.

jobStatusResponse200.summary: Response to a successful API call.

message.messageText:
0 out of 26 Fill Object File jobs are active. 
Cause . . . . . :   Out of 26 Fill Object File jobs, 0 jobs are active, 0 are on job queue, and 26 are on output queue.
Recovery  . . . :   No action is required. This is an informational message.

value.messageText: Unauthorized or expired token. Please login again.

applicationCode.description: Specify the application code to fill objects from.

environmentCode.description:
Specify the environment code in the selected application to fill objects from. 

*ALL   
All environment of the selected application are selected.

routeCode.description:
Specify the route code in the selected application to fill IFS objects from. The possible values are:  

*ALL   
All routes of the selected environment are selected.

*NONE  
No routes are used for the filling process.

Note: If you specify the blank value for this field, it will assume the value *NONE.

objectLibrary.description:
Specify the object library to fill objects from. The possible values are:   

*ALL   
All libraries of the selected environment are selected.  

*NONE  
No libraries  are used for the filling process.   

Note: If you specify the blank value for this field, it will assume the value *NONE.

objectCode.description:
Specify which objects must be selected. The possible values are: 

*ALL   
All objects in the selected libraries are processed.

\*generic\*   
Enter a generic object code name to be filled.

?wild?   
Enter a wild card for the objects to be filled.

objectType.description:
Specify the object type of the selected objects. The possible values are:  

*ALL  
All object types are processed.

Note: If you specify the blank value for this field, it will assume the value *NONE.

objectAttribute.description:
Specify the object attribute of the selected objects. The possible values are: 

*ALL    
All objects attributes are processed.

Note: If you specify the blank value for this field, it will assume the value *NONE.

request.description: Request data for the fill object file.

status.description: The return status which can be *NORM or *TERM. In case of *TERM look at the messages for more information.

response.description: This will return messages and status.

processingMode.description:
Specify the OS/400 environment to process this command. The possible values are: 

'1' = *INTERACTIVE   
The command is executed in your current job.

'2' = *FORCEBATCH   
The job is submitted to the batch environment using job description OMSJOBD.

processingMethod.description:
Specify the kind of action to perform on the selected objects. The possible values are: 

'0' = *FILL  
The selected objects are loaded in the TD/OMS database.  

'1' = *EMPTY   
The selected objects are emptied from the TD/OMS database.  

'2' = *REPORT   
A report is produced with the differences between the TD/OMS database and life situation. No information is stored in the TD/OMS database.

processOnlyRelations.description:
Specify if only the relationships of the selected objects have to be rebuild. The possible values are: 

'0' = *NO  
The selected objects are completely loaded in the TD/OMS database.  

'1' = *YES   
Only relationships of the selected objects are being rebuilt.

objectAndOr4GLGroup.description:
Specify if normal objects, 4GL objects or both have to be loaded. The possible values are: 

'0' = *BOTH   
Both 4GL and normal objects are being processed.

'1' = *OBJECT  
Only normal OS/400 objects are being processed.

'2' = *GROUP  
Only 4GL objects are being processed.

typeCA2ESelection.description:
Specify, for Synon objects only, if a specific area has to be selected for processing. The possible values are: 

'1' = *ANY  
All areas of the selected 4GL libraries are being processed.

'2' = *AREA   
Value *AREA indicates that keyword cA2EObjectCode contains the name of the application area to be processed.

'3' = *ANYSYS   
Name selection like keyword *ANY, but also system (template) objects defined internally in the model, will be part of the selection process.                                                            

'4' = *AREASYS   
Model Area selection like keyword *AREA, but also system (template) objects defined internally in the model, will be part of the selection process.

cA2EObjectCode.description:
Specify, for Synon objects only, the Synon object to be processed. The possible values are: 

*ALL
All Synon objects of the selected owning object are being processed.

Name   
Enter the name of an area if typeCA2ESelection contains *AREA or enter the name of a Synon object.

cA2EOwningObject.description:
Specify, for Synon objects only, the Synon object to be processed. The possible values are: 

*ALLOBJ   
Use this value when cA2EObjectCode contains *ALL

*ANY   
No selection is performed on the owning object code. 

Name   
Enter the owning object code name.

processingOptions.description: Various flags to control the behaviour of the API.

totalNumberOfJobs.description: Jobs of any status including jobs on job queues, active jobs, and jobs on an output queue.

jobsOnJobQueue.description: Jobs that are not active because they are waiting on a job queue.

jobsOnOutputQueue.description: Jobs that have completed execution and have output on an output queue.

jobStatusResponse.description: The response of the fill object file jobs that are submitted in batch.

fillObject.summary: How to specify the fill object file input parameters value for normal OS/400 objects.

fillObjectFor4GLObject.summary: How to specify the fill object file input parameters value for 4GL objects.
